Phase-71.NAME: Device Config Retrieval API and Config Page Master-Detail UX
Phase-71.summary: Approved plan for per-device VPN config retrieval, compact master-detail device UX, and user-cabinet polish without touching deploy/install/runtime topology.

scope.in: Authenticated read-only per-device config API, per-device .conf download, per-device QR/Amnezia QR rendering, frontend API methods, /dashboard/config master-detail UX, mobile sticky actions, QR modal close-icon polish, frameless cabinet logo, subscription calendar boundary pulse, logout/sidebar ordering, user-cabinet i18n cleanup, and copy removals requested by operator.
scope.out: Deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, TLS/DNS, VPN topology, MTProto runtime, billing catalog prices, YooKassa flow, database schema changes unless execution proves unavoidable, automatic config rotation on read, and secret-bearing logs.

data-flow.flow-1: User selects an existing active device in /dashboard/config.
data-flow.flow-3: Backend enforces current-user ownership through device policy and rejects revoked, blocked, missing, or foreign devices.
data-flow.flow-4: Backend loads the existing active VPN client through VPNService.get_device_client(device_id) and renders with VPNService.get_client_config(client).
data-flow.flow-5: Frontend detail panel exposes QR, .conf, and Copy for the selected device without rotate/create side effects.
data-flow.flow-6: Rotate and delete remain available only through a secondary menu and confirmation flow.

steps.step-1: Update MyGRACE module and verification contracts for Phase-71 planning boundaries.
steps.step-2: Add GET /api/v1/devices/{device_id}/config as read-only active-device config retrieval.
steps.step-3: Add GET /api/v1/devices/{device_id}/config/download using the existing mobile-safe .conf attachment helper.
steps.step-4: Add GET /api/v1/devices/{device_id}/config/qr using the existing QR payload renderer without printing config data.
steps.step-5: Add GET /api/v1/devices/{device_id}/config/qr/amnezia if the current Amnezia container helper can preserve exact config_data parity.
steps.step-6: Extend frontend deviceApi types and methods for getConfig, downloadConfig, getQRCode, and getAmneziaQRCode.
steps.step-7: Rebuild /dashboard/config into compact master-detail: header slots, device list rows, selected-device detail, QR/.conf/Copy primary actions, and disabled create state at device limit.
steps.step-8: Move rotate and revoke into a secondary ellipsis menu with confirmation and no adjacent destructive placement near primary QR/.conf/Copy actions.
steps.step-9: Implement mobile-safe config UX: compact list, tap-to-select detail, bottom or sticky QR/.conf/Copy actions, safe-area spacing, and no horizontal scroll.
steps.step-10: Replace the visible Закрыть button in the QR modal with an animated X icon button and remove the AmneziaVPN QR instruction text requested by operator.
steps.step-11: Remove the green square frame around the user-cabinet PNG logo while preserving transparent logo edges and asset budget boundaries.
steps.step-12: Add cyberpunk pulse/flicker borders for subscription rangeStart and rangeEnd calendar days with reduced-motion fallback.
steps.step-13: Move desktop Выйти below Настройки in the user sidebar ordering.
steps.step-14: Remove the backend-time explanatory copy from the trial/subscription card and replace Device-bound список with Список ваших устройств.
steps.step-15: Run an i18n pass for Settings, Config, SubscriptionPanel, Layout, and user-cabinet shared labels so English mode does not leak Russian copy for controlled interface strings.
steps.step-16: Run backend, frontend, smoke, XML, MyGRACE, diff, and protected-surface verification before marking execution complete.

acceptance.criterion-1: Existing active devices can be selected and their config, QR, and .conf downloaded without config_version changes or VPN peer rotation.
acceptance.criterion-2: Users with up to 9 devices can find, select, and export any device config on desktop and mobile without long awkward scrolling or hidden actions.
acceptance.criterion-3: Destructive device actions are secondary, confirmation-safe, and visually separated from QR/.conf/Copy.
acceptance.criterion-4: Logo, QR modal, calendar, logout order, subscription copy, devices title, and English settings translation issues match operator requests.

risks.risk-1: Config retrieval could accidentally reprovision or rotate; backend tests must assert config_version and peer identity are stable on GET.
risks.risk-2: Config, QR payload, private keys, and tokens are secret material; logs and smokes must stay marker-only and redacted.
risks.risk-3: Mobile master-detail or sticky actions can overlap bottom navigation; viewport smoke must cover phone and tablet sizes.
risks.risk-4: i18n cleanup may expose many hardcoded strings; execution should prioritize controlled user-cabinet strings and avoid broad unrelated rewrites.

verification-commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_device_api.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_trial_activation.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/plans/Phase-71.xml
mygrace lint; git diff --check; git diff --name-only -- install.sh deploy nginx docker-compose.yml deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy telegram-bot

backend-module-local.target-1: Device config endpoints must enforce current-user ownership and reject missing, foreign, revoked, blocked, inactive, and unprovisioned devices.
backend-module-local.target-2: GET config, download, QR, and optional Amnezia QR must be read-only: no config_version increment, no key rotation, no new device row, and no new peer provisioning.
backend-module-local.target-3: Selected-device config text must be rendered through the existing VPN service path so global config, per-device config, QR payload, and CLI parity stay aligned.
backend-module-local.target-4: Per-device .conf download must preserve application/octet-stream, attachment, filename and filename-star .conf metadata, nosniff, private/no-store headers, and no .conf.txt fallback.
backend-module-local.target-5: Pending trial first-config compatibility and device-limit policy remain unchanged by read-only retrieval endpoints.

frontend-module-local.target-1: /dashboard/config must use compact master-detail UX with device count, disabled create state at plan limit, selected-device detail, and primary QR/.conf/Copy actions.
frontend-module-local.target-2: Rotate and delete must be secondary menu actions with confirmation, separated from QR/.conf/Copy.
frontend-module-local.target-3: QR modal must use an animated icon-only X close button and must not show the removed AmneziaVPN scan instruction.
frontend-module-local.target-4: Frontend API methods must target selected devices and must not silently fall back to a global/current-device config endpoint.
frontend-module-local.target-5: Settings, Config, SubscriptionPanel, Layout, and shared cabinet labels must not leak controlled Russian strings in English mode.

visual-responsive-wave.target-1: User-cabinet logo must render transparent PNG edges without a green square frame.
visual-responsive-wave.target-2: Calendar rangeStart and rangeEnd days must have cyberpunk pulse/flicker boundaries with reduced-motion fallback.
visual-responsive-wave.target-3: Mobile views must support 1, 6, and 9 device rows without horizontal scroll, clipped text, bottom-nav overlap, or hidden sticky actions.
visual-responsive-wave.target-4: Desktop sidebar must place Выйти below Настройки and keep primary cabinet actions reachable without excessive scrolling.
visual-responsive-wave.target-5: All new buttons and icon controls must keep Matrix motion styling while preserving focus visibility and reduced-motion safety.

phase-level.gate-1: Backend device/vpn/trial tests must pass before API work can be called done-local.
phase-level.gate-2: Frontend production build and Phase-71 smoke must pass before UX work can be called done-local.
phase-level.gate-3: Phase-57, Phase-59, Phase-61, Phase-62, and Phase-70 regressions must stay green because Phase-71 touches their protected surfaces.
phase-level.gate-4: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff --check must pass.
phase-level.gate-5: Protected-surface guard must prove no deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/runtime/topology drift.

execution-evidence.backend: PASS: backend device/vpn/trial suite passed 31/31 with tests/test_device_api.py, tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py, and tests/test_trial_activation.py.
execution-evidence.frontend: PASS: frontend production build passed after Config master-detail, i18n, subscription calendar, layout logo, and settings updates.
execution-evidence.smokes: PASS: scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs and scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs passed locally; post-commit Phase-57, Phase-59, Phase-61, and Phase-62 regression smokes passed with protected backend/deploy/runtime guards clean.
execution-evidence.protected-surfaces: PASS: git diff --name-only over install.sh, deploy, nginx, docker-compose.yml, deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, .env.example, mtproto-runtime, official-mtproxy, telegram-bot, and frontend-admin returned empty.
execution-evidence.notes: Pre-commit Phase-57, Phase-59, Phase-61, and Phase-62 regression scripts initially failed only their own backend protected-diff guards because Phase-71 intentionally changed backend/app/devices/router.py and backend/tests/test_device_api.py; after commit those regression scripts passed cleanly.

telemetry-requirements.allowed: Route names, device counts, synthetic device IDs, redacted selected-device labels, viewport names, selector markers, response header names, screenshot paths, and pass/fail markers.
telemetry-requirements.forbidden: WireGuard private keys, preshared keys, full VPN configs, QR payload bodies, access tokens, refresh tokens, verify/reset tokens, MTProto secrets, tg proxy links with secrets, real user emails, IP history, payment IDs, Resend/YooKassa keys, and deployment secrets.
telemetry-requirements.redaction: Smoke tests and logs must prove behavior with synthetic fixtures or redacted identifiers only; live clean-deploy evidence must be operator-approved before any real user data is captured.

autonomous-execution-safety.allowed: Local execution may modify backend device/VPN read-only route code, frontend user-cabinet code, frontend i18n/CSS, focused tests, smoke scripts, and MyGRACE docs inside Phase-71 scope.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked: Stop and ask the operator before changing install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto runtime, VPN topology, TLS/DNS wiring, billing catalog prices, YooKassa flow, database schema, or secret-handling policy.
autonomous-execution-safety.handoff: If read-only per-device config requires schema changes or runtime topology changes, Phase-71 must be paused and split into a new plan instead of silently expanding scope.
